purpose: Final risk assessment — predicts closure probability using raw operational signals about current deal state, risk indicators, and urgency signals. Independent of the other 4 models.

features.reason: Current lead state (ON_HOLD, LOST, CLOSED, CONTRACT_SENT, etc.) — present state is the single strongest risk indicator
Profile completeness — incomplete deals at late stages are high-risk
Channel of origination — channel affects deal lifecycle and risk profile
Total days in pipeline — aged deals have higher drop-off risk
Days since last contact — the longer the silence, the higher the churn risk
Current sentiment label — negative sentiment at late stage is a strong churn signal
Depth of conversation — short conversations at late stage signal disengagement
Length of most recent message — very short last messages signal declining interest
Lead's industry — some industries (Oil & Gas, Healthcare) have different risk profiles and decision timelines
Whether a quotation exists — deals without quotation at late stage are structurally high-risk
Final deal value — very large deals carry higher risk of last-minute cancellation or delay
Enterprise deals have longer close cycles — risk assessment differs fundamentally from SMB
High revision count = prolonged negotiation = risk of deal fatigue and drop-off
Feedback on quotation — absence of feedback at quotation stage is a strong risk signal
Channel used to send quote — some channels have lower review and response rates
Response rate to follow-ups — low rate at late stage = high drop-off risk
Urgency language detected — presence of urgency indicates near-term decision likely
Price discussion in messages — active price talk signals the lead is close to deciding
Agent responsiveness — poor agent response at late stage increases churn risk significantly
Attachments exchanged — no attachments at proposal stage is a risk signal of low engagement
Installation cost — high installation costs with no buyer confirmation is a risk factor
Free installation offered — reduces friction; its absence at late stage can elevate deal risk
Product capacity — very large or very small capacity deals have different risk dynamics
Unit of capacity — required to correctly interpret capacity_value across product types
Bucketed capacity range — handles nonlinear risk patterns by deal size category
Product type attribute (centrifugal, portable, etc.) — complex product types carry higher deal risk
Base product price — price anchoring; extreme values signal unusual deal dynamics and risk
Tax rate applied — affects buyer's final cost perception and price sensitivity
